The band at 3401 cm-1 is ascribed to the -NH2 group [3]. The spectral features at 1254 cm^-1 and 1622 cm^-1 are attributed to the characteristic stretching vibration of the C-N bond in aromatic amines and the bending vibration of N-H, respectively, as noted in references [1-3]. Additionally, the peak noted at 3401 cm^-1 is associated with the amino group (-NH2), according to source [3]. Moreover, the spectrum of this sample displays distinct absorption bands characteristic of carboxylate groups ranging from 1380 to 1600 cm^-1, as well as absorption bands indicative of the O-Ti-O vibrations occurring between 400 and 800 cm^-1, as cited in sources [1-3].

The bands at 1254 and 1622 cm-1 are ascribed to the typical C-N stretching vibration of aromatic amine and the N-H bending vibration, respectively [1-3]. The band at 3401 cm-1 is ascribed to the -NH2 group [3]. Further, this specimen reveals typical bands of the carboxylate group at 1380-1600 cm-1 and the vibration adsorption of O-Ti-O bands at 400-800 cm-1 [1-3]  换一种表述

The absorption features observed at 1254 cm^-1 and 1622 cm^-1 correspond to the characteristic stretching of the C-N bond within aromatic amines and the bending of the N-H bond, respectively, as documented in references [1-3]. The presence of a peak at 3401 cm^-1 is indicative of the -NH2 functional group, specifically noted in source [3]. Additionally, the sample exhibits distinct absorption bands, which are representative of the carboxylate group and are found within the range of 1380 to 1600 cm^-1. Moreover, the sample demonstrates vibration absorption features associated with the O-Ti-O linkage, which are typically situated in the region of 400 to 800 cm^-1, as reported in references [1-3].

Conversely, the specimens 3M-3C--NML, 1M-5C--NML, and 1C-5M--NML did not display prominent characteristic peaks associated with Cu-O and Mn-O, indicating that the concentrations of copper and manganese ions within these samples were exceedingly low [4].

Hence, the FT-IR spectra of the samples showed similar characteristic peaks.  换一种表述
Consequently, the FT-IR spectral profiles of the samples exhibited analogous distinctive peaks.
analogous 和 similar 区别

The words "analogous" and "similar" both convey the idea of two or more items sharing certain characteristics or features, but there are subtle differences in their connotations and typical uses.

"Similar" is a more general term that means two or more things resemble each other but are not exactly the same. It suggests a degree of likeness that allows for comparison, so when you say things are similar, it means they have features in common, but they can also have significant differences.

"Analogous," on the other hand, tends to imply a deeper level of comparison. It is often used when the similarity in question involves functional or structural resemblance, especially in different contexts or systems. For example, in biology, the wings of a bat and the wings of a bird are analogous structures because they serve similar functions (flight), despite arising from different evolutionary paths. When you say things are analogous, you are generally suggesting a likeness in function or purpose, often despite superficial differences.

In the context of FT-IR spectra:
- Saying the spectra are "similar" would emphasize that the peaks are alike in appearance, position, etc., regardless of what the samples are.
- Saying they are "analogous" might suggest that the peaks not only appear alike but also correspond to similar molecular structures or bonds in the different samples. It could imply a closer level of comparison in terms of the molecular information being provided by the spectra.

In scientific writing, the distinction can be subtle and somewhat subjective, and in many cases, "similar" and "analogous" can be used interchangeably. However, "analogous" can carry a more precise implication of similarity in function or role, often in a comparative sense between different systems or contexts.
